articles.headline: Government Launches Phase 2 of National Logistics Policy to Enhance Supply Chain Efficiency
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government has officially rolled out the second phase of the National Logistics Policy (NLP), focusing on digitalization and multi-modal integration.
• This phase aims to reduce logistics costs from the current 13-14% of GDP to less than 8% by 2030, aligning with global benchmarks.
• Key initiatives include the expansion of the Unified Logistics Interface Platform (ULIP) and the integration of various digital systems like FASTag and e-Way Bill.
• Emphasis is placed on developing a robust multi-modal transport network, including dedicated freight corridors, port connectivity, and inland waterways.
• The policy is expected to significantly boost India's manufacturing competitiveness, exports, and 'Make in India' initiative.

Indian Economy and issues relating to Planning, Mobilization of Resources, Growth, Development and Employment.
Government Budgeting.
Investment models.

Focus areas: Digitalization, multi-modal connectivity, process re-engineering, skill development.
Nodal Ministry: Ministry of Commerce and Industry.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of reduced logistics costs for India's economic growth and global competitiveness.

Examine the potential impact of NLP on various sectors like manufacturing, agriculture, and e-commerce.
Identify challenges in implementation and suggest measures for effective execution of the policy.
articles.imageDescription: An infographic showing different modes of transport (road, rail, air, water) converging, representing multi-modal logistics.
articles.trivia: India's logistics sector is one of the largest employers, providing livelihoods to over 22 million people.

articles.headline: Supreme Court to Hear Review Petitions on Electoral Bonds Verdict
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court has announced it will hear a batch of review petitions challenging its recent verdict that struck down the Electoral Bonds scheme as unconstitutional.
• The petitions seek a reconsideration of the court's decision, citing concerns over potential implications for political funding transparency and donor privacy.
• The original verdict, delivered in February 2025, mandated the State Bank of India (SBI) to disclose details of electoral bond purchasers and recipient political parties.
• This development comes amidst ongoing public and political debate regarding the balance between transparency in political funding and the right to privacy of donors.
• Legal experts are closely watching the proceedings, as the outcome could further shape the future of political finance regulations in India.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
Role of Supreme Court in upholding constitutional principles.
Salient features of the Representation of People's Act.
prelimsPoints: Electoral Bonds scheme introduced in 2017.
Original verdict by a 5-judge Constitution Bench.
Key articles involved: Article 19(1)(a) (freedom of speech and expression), Article 21 (right to privacy).
Nodal bank for electoral bonds: State Bank of India (SBI).
Review petitions are filed under Article 137 of the Constitution.
mainsPoints: Analyze the constitutional principles invoked by the Supreme Court in striking down the Electoral Bonds scheme.
Discuss the arguments for and against the Electoral Bonds scheme, considering transparency, donor privacy, and political funding.
Evaluate the impact of the Supreme Court's original verdict on political finance in India.
Examine the implications of the review petitions for the future of electoral reforms and democratic accountability.
Propose alternative mechanisms for transparent and accountable political funding in India.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel and law books, symbolizing a Supreme Court hearing, with an electoral bond graphic in the background.
articles.trivia: The concept of 'anonymous' political donations has been a subject of debate globally, with various countries adopting different approaches to ensure both transparency and donor protection.

articles.headline: India and France Deepen Space Cooperation with New Joint Satellite Mission
articles.explainer:
• India (ISRO) and France (CNES) have announced a new joint satellite mission focused on climate change monitoring and oceanography.
• This collaboration builds upon a long-standing strategic partnership in space, including joint missions like Megha-Tropiques and Saral-Altika.
• The new mission will involve shared development of payloads and ground segments, aiming for enhanced data collection on critical environmental parameters.
• It underscores the growing importance of international cooperation in space for addressing global challenges like climate change and sustainable development.
• The partnership also includes knowledge sharing, training, and potential future collaborations on human spaceflight programs.

Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.

articles.trivia: India and France also collaborate on the 'Varuna' joint naval exercise, highlighting their comprehensive strategic partnership across multiple domains.

articles.headline: India's Green Hydrogen Mission Gains Momentum with New Investment Proposals
articles.explainer:
• The National Green Hydrogen Mission is reportedly receiving significant investment proposals from both domestic and international players.
• This surge in interest follows the government's recent policy incentives, including production-linked incentives (PLI) for electrolyzer manufacturing and green hydrogen production.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security and decarbonization goals.
• Several large-scale pilot projects for green hydrogen production and its derivatives (green ammonia, green methanol) are under various stages of implementation.
• Challenges remain in scaling up production, reducing costs, and developing robust infrastructure for storage and transportation.

articles.headline: Government Releases Draft AI Ethics Framework for Public Consultation
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has released a comprehensive draft framework on AI ethics and governance for public feedback.
• The framework emphasizes responsible AI development, focusing on principles like fairness, accountability, transparency, safety, and privacy.
• It aims to guide stakeholders across government, industry, and academia in building and deploying AI systems that align with India's values and regulatory landscape.
• Key areas addressed include data governance, algorithmic bias, human oversight, and the responsible use of AI in critical sectors.
• This move positions India among leading nations developing national strategies for ethical AI, fostering innovation while mitigating risks.

Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.

articles.headline: India-Pakistan Permanent Indus Commission Holds Annual Meeting
articles.explainer:
• The Permanent Indus Commission (PIC), comprising water commissioners from India and Pakistan, commenced its annual meeting in New Delhi.
• The talks focus on sharing of hydrological data, inspection of projects on both sides, and addressing technical objections to various water usages under the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT).
• This year's agenda includes discussions on India's hydroelectric projects on the Chenab river basin and Pakistan's concerns regarding their design.
• The IWT, brokered by the World Bank in 1960, is considered one of the most successful water-sharing agreements globally, despite bilateral tensions.
• The ongoing dialogue underscores the importance of continued engagement on shared water resources for regional stability.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Water disputes between states.
prelimsPoints: Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) signed in 1960.
Brokered by the World Bank.
Allocates Eastern Rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej) to India.
Allocates Western Rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab) to Pakistan, with India having limited non-consumptive usage rights.
Permanent Indus Commission (PIC) meets annually.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance and resilience of the Indus Waters Treaty amidst India-Pakistan geopolitical tensions.
Analyze the provisions of the IWT and how they manage water sharing between the two nations.
Evaluate the challenges and disputes that arise in the implementation of the IWT, particularly concerning hydroelectric projects.
Examine the role of the Permanent Indus Commission in facilitating dialogue and dispute resolution.
Discuss the broader implications of water-sharing agreements for regional cooperation and stability.
articles.imageDescription: A map showing the Indus River system with markers indicating India and Pakistan, symbolizing shared water resources.
articles.trivia: The Indus Waters Treaty is often cited as a model for transboundary water management, demonstrating how cooperation can persist even between adversarial nations.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflation Concerns
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) has decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive meeting.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach, balancing efforts to bring inflation within the target band while supporting economic growth.
• While retail inflation has shown signs of moderation, the MPC highlighted persistent risks from volatile food prices and global commodity fluctuations.
• The RBI reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the 4% target on a durable basis.
• Experts anticipate that the RBI might consider rate cuts later in the year, contingent on sustained disinflationary trends and stable economic indicators.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to Planning, Mobilization of Resources, Growth, Development and Employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary Policy.
prelimsPoints: Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of 6 members (3 RBI, 3 GoI).
RBI's inflation target: 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Reverse repo rate is the rate at which RBI borrows money from commercial banks.
Withdrawal of accommodation' stance implies reducing liquidity from the system.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and tools of India's monetary policy, focusing on inflation targeting.
Analyze the rationale behind the RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate amidst current economic conditions.
Evaluate the impact of monetary policy decisions on economic growth, inflation, and financial stability.
Examine the challenges faced by the MPC in balancing inflation control with growth imperatives.
Discuss the potential future trajectory of India's monetary policy based on domestic and global economic factors.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting an interest rate curve and a graph showing inflation trends, with the RBI logo.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was established in India in 2016, marking a significant shift towards a more transparent and accountable monetary policy framework.

articles.headline: Ministry of Education Reviews Progress of National Education Policy 2020 Implementation
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education held a high-level meeting to review the implementation status of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 across states and union territories.
• Key focus areas of the review included the integration of foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN), curriculum reforms, teacher training, and digital education initiatives.
• While significant progress has been reported in certain areas, challenges persist, particularly in the effective rollout of multi-disciplinary education and vocational training at school levels.
• The NEP 2020 aims for holistic and multidisciplinary education, skill development, and increased access to quality education for all.
• The ministry urged states to accelerate reforms to meet the policy's ambitious targets for transforming India's education landscape.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry —the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.

New school curricular structure: 5+3+3+4.

articles.trivia: The Kothari Commission (1964-66) was instrumental in shaping India's first National Policy on Education in 1968, advocating for a common school system and 6% of GDP spending on education.

articles.headline: Government Expands Climate-Resilient Agriculture Initiatives in Vulnerable Regions
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare announced the expansion of its climate-resilient agriculture program to cover more drought-prone and flood-affected districts.
• The initiative focuses on promoting stress-tolerant crop varieties, efficient water management techniques, and climate-smart farming practices.
• It integrates traditional knowledge with modern technology to help farmers adapt to changing weather patterns and ensure food security.
• Funding for the expansion will come from enhanced allocations under existing schemes like Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana (PKVY) and Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana (PMKSY).
• The program also includes capacity building and awareness campaigns for farmers on climate change impacts and adaptation strategies.

articles.headline: India's Semiconductor Mission Attracts Global Players, Boosts Domestic Manufacturing
articles.explainer:
• India's Semiconductor Mission has seen significant progress, with several global semiconductor giants announcing plans for fabrication units and assembly, testing, marking, and packaging (ATMP) facilities.
• The government's production-linked incentive (PLI) scheme for semiconductors and display manufacturing is a major catalyst for these investments.
• This initiative aims to establish a robust domestic semiconductor ecosystem, reducing reliance on imports and enhancing strategic autonomy.
• The focus is on attracting investments across the entire value chain, from design to manufacturing and packaging.
• Skill development and R&D support are also crucial components to build a sustainable semiconductor industry in India.

articles.headline: Namami Gange Mission Phase II Focuses on Tributary Rejuvenation and Decentralized Treatment
articles.explainer:
• The second phase of the Namami Gange Mission is intensifying its efforts, with a renewed focus on cleaning the tributaries of the Ganga river and promoting decentralized wastewater treatment.
• This phase emphasizes a 'ridge to valley' approach, addressing pollution sources from the upper catchments down to the main river.
• Key strategies include the construction of more Sewage Treatment Plants (STPs) in smaller towns along tributaries and promoting natural treatment systems.
• Community participation and public awareness campaigns are being enhanced to ensure the sustainability of the rejuvenation efforts.
• The mission also integrates biodiversity conservation, afforestation, and wetlands restoration along the Ganga basin.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.

Nodal Ministry: Ministry of Jal Shakti.
NMCG (National Mission for Clean Ganga) is the implementation arm.
Key pillars: Sewerage infrastructure, industrial pollution abatement, river front development, river surface cleaning, biodiversity conservation, afforestation, public awareness.
Focus on 'Aviral Dhara' (uninterrupted flow) and 'Nirmal Dhara' (clean flow).
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and achievements of the Namami Gange Mission in its first phase.

articles.headline: Expert Panel Recommends Enhanced Financial Autonomy for Urban Local Bodies
articles.explainer:
• An expert committee appointed by the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs has submitted recommendations for strengthening the financial autonomy of Urban Local Bodies (ULBs).
• The report suggests measures such as property tax reforms, user charges for services, municipal bond issuance, and improved inter-governmental fiscal transfers.
• It highlights the critical need for ULBs to generate their own resources to fund urban infrastructure development and service delivery.
• The recommendations aim to enhance the capacity of ULBs to implement smart city projects and provide better civic amenities.
• This move is crucial for addressing the increasing urbanization challenges and ensuring sustainable urban development in India.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies.
prelimsPoints: 74th Constitutional Amendment Act (1992) gave constitutional status to ULBs.
12th Schedule lists 18 functions of municipalities.

Municipal bonds are debt instruments issued by ULBs.

Analyze the significance of increased financial autonomy for ULBs in improving urban governance and service delivery.
Evaluate the proposed measures like property tax reforms and municipal bonds in enhancing ULB revenue generation.
Examine the role of state and central governments in facilitating fiscal decentralization to urban local bodies.
Suggest comprehensive reforms to ensure sustainable financing and effective functioning of ULBs in the context of rapid urbanization.
articles.imageDescription: A city skyline with a financial graph superimposed, representing urban finance and development.
articles.trivia: Mumbai was the first city in India to issue municipal bonds in 1997, followed by Ahmedabad.

articles.headline: India Assumes Chairmanship of UN Statistical Commission, Underscores Data-Driven Governance
articles.explainer:
• India has officially assumed the chairmanship of the United Nations Statistical Commission for a four-year term, effective from March 2025.
• This marks a significant recognition of India's robust statistical infrastructure and its commitment to data-driven policy-making and sustainable development.
• The role involves leading global efforts in setting statistical standards, coordinating international statistical activities, and promoting data sharing.
• India plans to leverage this position to advocate for enhanced statistical capacities in developing countries and to highlight the importance of timely and reliable data for achieving SDGs.
• The chairmanship reflects India's growing influence in multilateral forums and its expertise in leveraging big data for governance and development.

articles.headline: Animal Welfare Board Issues New Guidelines for Protection of Street Animals
articles.explainer:
• The Animal Welfare Board of India (AWBI) has issued a comprehensive set of new guidelines aimed at enhancing the protection and welfare of street animals across the country.
• The guidelines emphasize responsible pet ownership, promotion of animal birth control (ABC) programs, and establishing helplines for rescue and reporting cruelty.
• They also call for stricter enforcement of existing laws against animal cruelty and suggest collaboration between local authorities, NGOs, and community volunteers.
• This move comes in response to increasing concerns over human-animal conflict and the need for a more humane approach to managing stray animal populations.
• The AWBI has urged state governments and local bodies to adopt and implement these guidelines effectively.

articles.headline: INS Vikrant Successfully Completes Advanced Aviation Facility Trials
articles.explainer:
• India's first indigenously designed and built aircraft carrier, INS Vikrant, has successfully completed its advanced aviation facility (AAF) trials.
• These trials involved testing the flight deck, aircraft launch and recovery systems, and various combat readiness systems with different aircraft types.
• The successful completion marks a crucial step towards the carrier's full operational capability, significantly enhancing the Indian Navy's blue-water capabilities.
• INS Vikrant showcases India's growing self-reliance in defense manufacturing and complex shipbuilding capabilities under the 'Make in India' initiative.
• The carrier is expected to host a mix of MiG-29K fighter jets, Kamov-31 helicopters, MH-60R multi-role helicopters, and eventually, indigenous fighter aircraft.

articles.headline: Ministry of Tourism Launches 'Virasat Darshan' Initiative to Boost Heritage Tourism
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism has launched a new initiative, 'Virasat Darshan' (Heritage Sightseeing), aimed at promoting India's rich cultural and historical heritage.
• The program focuses on developing curated heritage circuits, improving visitor amenities, and leveraging digital platforms for immersive experiences.
• It seeks to attract both domestic and international tourists, thereby boosting local economies and creating employment opportunities in heritage sites.
• Special emphasis is placed on lesser-known heritage locations and intangible cultural heritage, integrating local communities into the tourism value chain.
• The initiative aligns with the 'Dekho Apna Desh' campaign and aims to position India as a premier global heritage tourism destination.

articles.headline: New National Policy for Senior Citizens Focuses on Integrated Elderly Care
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment has unveiled a draft of the new National Policy for Senior Citizens, emphasizing integrated care and support systems.
• The policy aims to address the multi-faceted needs of the elderly, including health, financial security, housing, and protection against abuse.
• It proposes a shift from a welfare-based approach to a rights-based framework, ensuring dignity and active participation of senior citizens in society.
• Key provisions include strengthening geriatric healthcare services, promoting digital literacy, and expanding social security schemes.
• The policy also encourages intergenerational bonding and provides support for caregivers, recognizing the demographic shift towards an aging population.

articles.headline: Indian Cities Adopt Green Infrastructure to Combat Urban Heat Island Effect
articles.explainer:
• Several Indian cities are actively implementing green infrastructure projects, such as urban forests, green roofs, and permeable pavements, to mitigate the urban heat island (UHI) effect.
• The UHI effect causes urban areas to be significantly warmer than surrounding rural areas due to human activities and materials like concrete and asphalt.
• These initiatives aim to reduce ambient temperatures, improve air quality, enhance biodiversity, and promote water conservation.
• Projects are being supported through various urban development schemes and partnerships with environmental organizations.
• Challenges include limited urban space, high implementation costs, and the need for sustained maintenance and public participation.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Disaster and disaster management.
prelimsPoints: UHI effect is caused by heat absorption by urban surfaces and anthropogenic heat generation.
Consequences: Increased energy consumption, elevated greenhouse gas emissions, heat-related illnesses.
Mitigation strategies: Green roofs, cool pavements, urban forests, reflective surfaces.
Smart Cities Mission encourages green infrastructure.
National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) also indirectly benefits from UHI mitigation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the causes and consequences of the Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect in rapidly urbanizing Indian cities.
Analyze how green infrastructure solutions can effectively mitigate the UHI effect and contribute to urban sustainability.
Evaluate the challenges faced by urban planners and local authorities in implementing green infrastructure projects.
Examine the co-benefits of green infrastructure for air quality, biodiversity, and public health.
Suggest integrated urban planning strategies to build climate-resilient and livable cities in India.
articles.imageDescription: A split image showing a concrete city on one side (hot) and a green city with trees and parks on the other (cool), illustrating UHI mitigation.
articles.trivia: A study found that a 10% increase in tree canopy cover can reduce ambient air temperatures by 1-2 degrees Celsius.

articles.headline: Government Unveils Policy to Boost Startup Ecosystem in Tier-2 and Tier-3 Cities
articles.explainer:
• The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) has launched a new policy framework aimed at fostering a vibrant startup ecosystem in India's Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities.
• The policy provides incentives such as seed funding, mentorship programs, incubation support, and easier access to venture capital for startups outside metropolitan areas.
• It seeks to decentralize the startup growth, unlock entrepreneurial potential in smaller cities, and promote equitable regional development.
• Emphasis is also placed on developing sector-specific incubators and leveraging local talent pools in emerging tech and traditional industries.
• This initiative is crucial for realizing the vision of 'Startup India' beyond the major urban centers, creating jobs and driving innovation locally.

mainsPoints: Discuss the role of startups in driving economic growth, innovation, and job creation in India.
Analyze the need for a focused policy to promote startups in Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities, beyond major metros.

Examine the challenges faced by startups in smaller cities, including access to funding, mentorship, and talent.
Suggest comprehensive strategies to build a robust and inclusive startup ecosystem that spans across all regions of India.

articles.headline: India Pledges Enhanced Role in Global Health Security and Pandemic Preparedness
articles.explainer:
• India has reiterated its commitment to playing a more significant role in strengthening global health security and enhancing pandemic preparedness at an international forum.
• The commitment includes sharing vaccine development expertise, manufacturing capabilities, and providing affordable medical supplies to developing nations.
• India emphasized the need for equitable access to healthcare resources and a collaborative approach to address future health crises.
• This stance aligns with India's 'Vaccine Maitri' initiative and its growing status as a global pharmaceutical hub.
• The focus is on building resilient health systems, promoting research and development, and strengthening surveillance mechanisms worldwide.

WHO is the specialized agency of the UN responsible for international public health.

Analyze the significance of India's pledge to enhance global health security and pandemic preparedness.
Evaluate India's strengths as a pharmaceutical hub and vaccine manufacturer in addressing global health crises.

Suggest ways India can further leverage its capabilities to build a more resilient and equitable global health architecture.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Logistics Policy (NLP), consider the following statements:
1. It aims to reduce logistics costs to less than 8% of GDP by 2030.
2. The Unified Logistics Interface Platform (ULIP) is a key digital initiative under this policy.
3. The policy primarily focuses on road transport infrastructure development.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The NLP aims to reduce logistics costs from 13-14% of GDP to less than 8% by 2030. Statement 2 is correct: ULIP is a crucial digital platform under NLP for integrating various logistics stakeholders. Statement 3 is incorrect: The policy emphasizes multi-modal transport development, including road, rail, air, and waterways, not just road transport.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for establishing statistical standards and coordinating international statistical activities at the global level?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The United Nations Statistical Commission is the highest body of the global statistical system. It is responsible for setting statistical standards, developing concepts and methods, and coordinating international statistical activities.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the National Green Hydrogen Mission:
1. Its primary objective is to make India a net exporter of green hydrogen.
2. The mission targets annual green hydrogen production of 5 million tonnes by 2030.
3. Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes are a part of the mission's strategy.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export (Statement 1). It targets annual production of 5 million tonnes by 2030 (Statement 2). PLI schemes for electrolyzer manufacturing and green hydrogen production are key components of the mission (Statement 3).

prelimsMocks.question:
The Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect is a phenomenon where urban areas experience significantly warmer temperatures than surrounding rural areas. Which of the following are potential strategies to mitigate the UHI effect?
1. Increasing green spaces and urban forests.
2. Using reflective surfaces for roofs and pavements.
3. Promoting permeable pavements.
4. Expanding the use of concrete and asphalt.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statements 1, 2, and 3 are correct strategies to mitigate the UHI effect. Green spaces, reflective surfaces (cool roofs/pavements), and permeable pavements help reduce heat absorption and increase evaporative cooling. Statement 4 is incorrect; concrete and asphalt are major contributors to the UHI effect due to their heat-absorbing properties.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a fundamental duty of every citizen of India as per the Constitution?
options: (a) To abide by the Constitution and respect its ideals and institutions.
(b) To protect and improve the natural environment.
(c) To pay taxes to the government promptly.
(d) To safeguard public property and to abjure violence.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) To pay taxes to the government promptly.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Paying taxes is a legal obligation but is not explicitly listed as a Fundamental Duty under Article 51-A of the Indian Constitution. The other options are Fundamental Duties: (a) is 51-A(a), (b) is 51-A(g), and (d) is 51-A(i).

mainsMocks.question: Critically analyze the efficacy of the National Logistics Policy in achieving its stated objectives of reducing logistics costs and boosting India's global competitiveness. What challenges might hinder its full implementation, and how can they be addressed?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to Planning, Mobilization of Resources, Growth, Development and Employment; Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.

mainsMocks.question: The recent Supreme Court verdict on Electoral Bonds has sparked a crucial debate on transparency versus donor privacy in political funding. Discuss the constitutional arguments involved and evaluate the implications of such rulings for democratic accountability in India.

Analyze how undisclosed funding impacts free and fair elections, accountability, and potential quid pro quo. Conclude with the broader implications for electoral reforms and strengthening democratic institutions.

mainsMocks.approach: Start by highlighting the demographic shift towards an aging population in India and the associated challenges (health, financial, social isolation). Discuss the key features of the new policy, focusing on integrated care, rights-based approach, and specific interventions. Critically evaluate its potential strengths and weaknesses. Conclude by suggesting additional measures such as robust social security, geriatric care infrastructure, digital inclusion, and intergenerational programs for active aging.

GS1.headline: Ministry of Tourism Launches 'Virasat Darshan' Initiative to Boost Heritage Tourism
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism has launched a new initiative, 'Virasat Darshan' (Heritage Sightseeing), aimed at promoting India's rich cultural and historical heritage.
• The program focuses on developing curated heritage circuits, improving visitor amenities, and leveraging digital platforms for immersive experiences.
• It seeks to attract both domestic and international tourists, thereby boosting local economies and creating employment opportunities in heritage sites.
• Special emphasis is placed on lesser-known heritage locations and intangible cultural heritage, integrating local communities into the tourism value chain.
• The initiative aligns with the 'Dekho Apna Desh' campaign and aims to position India as a premier global heritage tourism destination.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court to Hear Review Petitions on Electoral Bonds Verdict
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court has announced it will hear a batch of review petitions challenging its recent verdict that struck down the Electoral Bonds scheme as unconstitutional.
• The petitions seek a reconsideration of the court's decision, citing concerns over potential implications for political funding transparency and donor privacy.
• The original verdict, delivered in February 2025, mandated the State Bank of India (SBI) to disclose details of electoral bond purchasers and recipient political parties.
• This development comes amidst ongoing public and political debate regarding the balance between transparency in political funding and the right to privacy of donors.
• Legal experts are closely watching the proceedings, as the outcome could further shape the future of political finance regulations in India.

Role of Supreme Court in upholding constitutional principles.

prelimsPoints: Electoral Bonds scheme introduced in 2017.
Original verdict by a 5-judge Constitution Bench.
Key articles involved: Article 19(1)(a) (freedom of speech and expression), Article 21 (right to privacy).
Nodal bank for electoral bonds: State Bank of India (SBI).
Review petitions are filed under Article 137 of the Constitution.
mainsPoints: Analyze the constitutional principles invoked by the Supreme Court in striking down the Electoral Bonds scheme.
Discuss the arguments for and against the Electoral Bonds scheme, considering transparency, donor privacy, and political funding.

GS2.headline: India and France Deepen Space Cooperation with New Joint Satellite Mission
GS2.explainer:

• This collaboration builds upon a long-standing strategic partnership in space, including joint missions like Megha-Tropiques and Saral-Altika.
• The new mission will involve shared development of payloads and ground segments, aiming for enhanced data collection on critical environmental parameters.
• It underscores the growing importance of international cooperation in space for addressing global challenges like climate change and sustainable development.
• The partnership also includes knowledge sharing, training, and potential future collaborations on human spaceflight programs.

GS2.headline: India-Pakistan Permanent Indus Commission Holds Annual Meeting
GS2.explainer:
• The Permanent Indus Commission (PIC), comprising water commissioners from India and Pakistan, commenced its annual meeting in New Delhi.
• The talks focus on sharing of hydrological data, inspection of projects on both sides, and addressing technical objections to various water usages under the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT).
• This year's agenda includes discussions on India's hydroelectric projects on the Chenab river basin and Pakistan's concerns regarding their design.
• The IWT, brokered by the World Bank in 1960, is considered one of the most successful water-sharing agreements globally, despite bilateral tensions.
• The ongoing dialogue underscores the importance of continued engagement on shared water resources for regional stability.

GS2.headline: Ministry of Education Reviews Progress of National Education Policy 2020 Implementation
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education held a high-level meeting to review the implementation status of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 across states and union territories.
• Key focus areas of the review included the integration of foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN), curriculum reforms, teacher training, and digital education initiatives.
• While significant progress has been reported in certain areas, challenges persist, particularly in the effective rollout of multi-disciplinary education and vocational training at school levels.
• The NEP 2020 aims for holistic and multidisciplinary education, skill development, and increased access to quality education for all.
• The ministry urged states to accelerate reforms to meet the policy's ambitious targets for transforming India's education landscape.

GS2.explainer:

• The report suggests measures such as property tax reforms, user charges for services, municipal bond issuance, and improved inter-governmental fiscal transfers.

• The recommendations aim to enhance the capacity of ULBs to implement smart city projects and provide better civic amenities.
• This move is crucial for addressing the increasing urbanization challenges and ensuring sustainable urban development in India.

• The role involves leading global efforts in setting statistical standards, coordinating international statistical activities, and promoting data sharing.
• India plans to leverage this position to advocate for enhanced statistical capacities in developing countries and to highlight the importance of timely and reliable data for achieving SDGs.
• The chairmanship reflects India's growing influence in multilateral forums and its expertise in leveraging big data for governance and development.

GS2.headline: Animal Welfare Board Issues New Guidelines for Protection of Street Animals
GS2.explainer:
• The Animal Welfare Board of India (AWBI) has issued a comprehensive set of new guidelines aimed at enhancing the protection and welfare of street animals across the country.
• The guidelines emphasize responsible pet ownership, promotion of animal birth control (ABC) programs, and establishing helplines for rescue and reporting cruelty.
• They also call for stricter enforcement of existing laws against animal cruelty and suggest collaboration between local authorities, NGOs, and community volunteers.
• This move comes in response to increasing concerns over human-animal conflict and the need for a more humane approach to managing stray animal populations.
• The AWBI has urged state governments and local bodies to adopt and implement these guidelines effectively.

GS2.headline: India Pledges Enhanced Role in Global Health Security and Pandemic Preparedness
GS2.explainer:
• India has reiterated its commitment to playing a more significant role in strengthening global health security and enhancing pandemic preparedness at an international forum.
• The commitment includes sharing vaccine development expertise, manufacturing capabilities, and providing affordable medical supplies to developing nations.
• India emphasized the need for equitable access to healthcare resources and a collaborative approach to address future health crises.
• This stance aligns with India's 'Vaccine Maitri' initiative and its growing status as a global pharmaceutical hub.
• The focus is on building resilient health systems, promoting research and development, and strengthening surveillance mechanisms worldwide.

GS3.headline: Government Launches Phase 2 of National Logistics Policy to Enhance Supply Chain Efficiency
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government has officially rolled out the second phase of the National Logistics Policy (NLP), focusing on digitalization and multi-modal integration.
• This phase aims to reduce logistics costs from the current 13-14% of GDP to less than 8% by 2030, aligning with global benchmarks.
• Key initiatives include the expansion of the Unified Logistics Interface Platform (ULIP) and the integration of various digital systems like FASTag and e-Way Bill.
• Emphasis is placed on developing a robust multi-modal transport network, including dedicated freight corridors, port connectivity, and inland waterways.
• The policy is expected to significantly boost India's manufacturing competitiveness, exports, and 'Make in India' initiative.

GS3.headline: India's Green Hydrogen Mission Gains Momentum with New Investment Proposals
GS3.explainer:
• The National Green Hydrogen Mission is reportedly receiving significant investment proposals from both domestic and international players.
• This surge in interest follows the government's recent policy incentives, including production-linked incentives (PLI) for electrolyzer manufacturing and green hydrogen production.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security and decarbonization goals.
• Several large-scale pilot projects for green hydrogen production and its derivatives (green ammonia, green methanol) are under various stages of implementation.
• Challenges remain in scaling up production, reducing costs, and developing robust infrastructure for storage and transportation.

GS3.headline: Government Releases Draft AI Ethics Framework for Public Consultation
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has released a comprehensive draft framework on AI ethics and governance for public feedback.
• The framework emphasizes responsible AI development, focusing on principles like fairness, accountability, transparency, safety, and privacy.
• It aims to guide stakeholders across government, industry, and academia in building and deploying AI systems that align with India's values and regulatory landscape.
• Key areas addressed include data governance, algorithmic bias, human oversight, and the responsible use of AI in critical sectors.
• This move positions India among leading nations developing national strategies for ethical AI, fostering innovation while mitigating risks.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflation Concerns
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) has decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive meeting.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach, balancing efforts to bring inflation within the target band while supporting economic growth.
• While retail inflation has shown signs of moderation, the MPC highlighted persistent risks from volatile food prices and global commodity fluctuations.
• The RBI reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the 4% target on a durable basis.
• Experts anticipate that the RBI might consider rate cuts later in the year, contingent on sustained disinflationary trends and stable economic indicators.

GS3.headline: Government Expands Climate-Resilient Agriculture Initiatives in Vulnerable Regions
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare announced the expansion of its climate-resilient agriculture program to cover more drought-prone and flood-affected districts.
• The initiative focuses on promoting stress-tolerant crop varieties, efficient water management techniques, and climate-smart farming practices.
• It integrates traditional knowledge with modern technology to help farmers adapt to changing weather patterns and ensure food security.
• Funding for the expansion will come from enhanced allocations under existing schemes like Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana (PKVY) and Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana (PMKSY).
• The program also includes capacity building and awareness campaigns for farmers on climate change impacts and adaptation strategies.

GS3.headline: India's Semiconductor Mission Attracts Global Players, Boosts Domestic Manufacturing
GS3.explainer:
• India's Semiconductor Mission has seen significant progress, with several global semiconductor giants announcing plans for fabrication units and assembly, testing, marking, and packaging (ATMP) facilities.

• This initiative aims to establish a robust domestic semiconductor ecosystem, reducing reliance on imports and enhancing strategic autonomy.
• The focus is on attracting investments across the entire value chain, from design to manufacturing and packaging.
• Skill development and R&D support are also crucial components to build a sustainable semiconductor industry in India.

GS3.headline: Namami Gange Mission Phase II Focuses on Tributary Rejuvenation and Decentralized Treatment
GS3.explainer:
• The second phase of the Namami Gange Mission is intensifying its efforts, with a renewed focus on cleaning the tributaries of the Ganga river and promoting decentralized wastewater treatment.
• This phase emphasizes a 'ridge to valley' approach, addressing pollution sources from the upper catchments down to the main river.
• Key strategies include the construction of more Sewage Treatment Plants (STPs) in smaller towns along tributaries and promoting natural treatment systems.
• Community participation and public awareness campaigns are being enhanced to ensure the sustainability of the rejuvenation efforts.
• The mission also integrates biodiversity conservation, afforestation, and wetlands restoration along the Ganga basin.

GS3.headline: INS Vikrant Successfully Completes Advanced Aviation Facility Trials
GS3.explainer:
• India's first indigenously designed and built aircraft carrier, INS Vikrant, has successfully completed its advanced aviation facility (AAF) trials.
• These trials involved testing the flight deck, aircraft launch and recovery systems, and various combat readiness systems with different aircraft types.
• The successful completion marks a crucial step towards the carrier's full operational capability, significantly enhancing the Indian Navy's blue-water capabilities.
• INS Vikrant showcases India's growing self-reliance in defense manufacturing and complex shipbuilding capabilities under the 'Make in India' initiative.
• The carrier is expected to host a mix of MiG-29K fighter jets, Kamov-31 helicopters, MH-60R multi-role helicopters, and eventually, indigenous fighter aircraft.

GS3.headline: Indian Cities Adopt Green Infrastructure to Combat Urban Heat Island Effect
GS3.explainer:
• Several Indian cities are actively implementing green infrastructure projects, such as urban forests, green roofs, and permeable pavements, to mitigate the urban heat island (UHI) effect.
• The UHI effect causes urban areas to be significantly warmer than surrounding rural areas due to human activities and materials like concrete and asphalt.
• These initiatives aim to reduce ambient temperatures, improve air quality, enhance biodiversity, and promote water conservation.
• Projects are being supported through various urban development schemes and partnerships with environmental organizations.
• Challenges include limited urban space, high implementation costs, and the need for sustained maintenance and public participation.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils Policy to Boost Startup Ecosystem in Tier-2 and Tier-3 Cities
GS3.explainer:
• The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) has launched a new policy framework aimed at fostering a vibrant startup ecosystem in India's Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities.
• The policy provides incentives such as seed funding, mentorship programs, incubation support, and easier access to venture capital for startups outside metropolitan areas.
• It seeks to decentralize the startup growth, unlock entrepreneurial potential in smaller cities, and promote equitable regional development.
• Emphasis is also placed on developing sector-specific incubators and leveraging local talent pools in emerging tech and traditional industries.
• This initiative is crucial for realizing the vision of 'Startup India' beyond the major urban centers, creating jobs and driving innovation locally.
